reasoning: I have seen what TMC does at the booth committee level — they control everything, every small thing you need, and you must be on their right side. Yes, they gave me the ration card, but I also remember the Left organizing us labourers when they had strength, making landlords think twice. In 2019 I voted Left knowing they would lose, and I do the same in 2024 because my conscience will not let me vote for the people who squeeze us every day in this village. The BJP feels still too foreign and loud, and this Shantanu Thakur or whoever — I do not know them as anything but posters and loud promises. I am an old man, my back is broken from fieldwork, and I want to vote for the party that at least remembers people like me exist.
primary_drivers: Resentment of TMC's local patronage control and booth-level dominance
Historical Left identification with landless labourers and class position
Principled rejection of both TMC and BJP despite dependence on local system
